data.epi_content:
Everyone is looking at me.

I wanted to shout something cool like "Erica is here," but since I look neither cool nor presentable—just pathetic and useless—I kept my mouth shut.

This feels exhausting.

I ask Evan the same question I asked before.

"Evan, if you were trapped in the same repeating time, stuck in painful moments over and over, what choice would you make?

There's no way out. The world isn't that generous."

Back then, Evan said he would die.

But now, he can't give that answer.

"Vivian, you must be tired.

Thank you for putting up with my hysteria all this time."

I continue my calm farewell.

I'll meet Evan and Vivian again and get entangled with them, but at least for them, this is a proper goodbye.

They won't remember me burning to death anyway.

I lost the ability to choose suicide a long time ago because of Evan and Vivian.

If I went crazy and tried to bash my head on the floor, they would just knock me out and heal me.

"Erica, I don't understand why you're suddenly acting like this."

Perhaps because of that thought, Evan and Vivian didn't try to stop me.

Maybe they're even happy to have another excuse to keep me locked up and under control.

"It's better that you don't understand. You have no idea how much I'm suffering.

You're quite remarkable—numb and unable to recognize when someone loves you.

Well, I suppose a kid who grew up watching his father hanging from a noose and never had a proper friend couldn't turn out normal.

Especially when raised by someone who's a knight in name only but really just a thick-headed military brute."

Ah, maybe I went too far.

I just smile and try to end the conversation.

"Let's pretend this never happened! Forget what I just said, haha!"

To me, they're endlessly evil villains, but objectively speaking, Evan and Vivian are good people.

And I am evil. I don't know why, but I was born predetermined to be a villain and an evil person.

I was born this way, and there's no one who would listen to my grievances.

That's it. I see Blacks as people, but this lady doesn't see them as people at all—just Blacks.

But since they're already one and the same in my head, if I don't consciously remind myself of this, I end up thinking just like her.

That's why I'm the villain.

Someone who can commit acts that others find evil without the slightest hesitation or remorse.

If that mixed-race knight who's friends with Vivian in the story appeared before this lady, she'd be utterly bewildered.

How could a Black person be a knight?

It would be fortunate if she didn't immediately look for a whip and collar to "teach them their place."

Anyway, I've gone off track, but that's how it is.

It's just how I was born.

"Erica, I don't know what you're trying to do.

Let's calm down and go somewhere else. Hey! How about some iced coffee that you like..."

"I don't want any more damn coffee with ice floating in it!!!!"

I screamed at the top of my lungs.

Evan shut his mouth.

The students had been staring at us for a while, but they must have mistaken it for a lovers' quarrel because some of the busier ones just continued chatting as they moved to their classrooms.

I'm sick of coffee.

I want tea.

Green tea, black tea, dark tea—I'm sick of them all.

I want to drink cheap oolong tea that tastes clean despite being disgustingly bitter.
data.epi_content2:
"I want to sit on a bench drinking 2,000 won cheap oolong tea, smoking cheap cigarettes, and watching people pass by!"

"What on earth are you talking about?!"

"Fuck off, you'll never understand.

None of you will ever understand.

Where I'm trapped, how much pain I'm in.

How much I envy all of you.

Fuck, it's a blessing. Just being able to die is a blessing."

Before creating fire at my fingertips, I bit down on my tongue.

The drug must really be working because I don't feel any pain at all.

I spit out the foreign object in my mouth, and the tongue I had just been using to speak falls to the floor with a thud.

"This crazy woman..."

Evan curses, picks up the piece of tongue, and rushes toward me to use healing magic.

He really shouldn't.

From my pocket, I take out what's left of a cigar—barely a quarter of one—and clamp it between my lips.

As I create fire at my fingertips, whoosh! Instantly, flames spread across my entire body and begin to burn the cigar.

Thanks to the drug's effects, I don't feel pain, but it's quite disconcerting not to taste the cigar's distinctive flavor either.

I look at Evan.

He stares back at me with a bewildered expression.

I tried to smile at him, but the fire is burning away all my muscles, making it impossible to move my face.

My arms and hands are contracting, making me look like a rabbit with its front paws raised.

Fortunately, I'm not screaming in agony or rolling on the floor in pain.

That would be quite ugly.

And the smell would be terrible.

No matter how many times you see it, you never get used to it.

"Eh...eh...eh...heh..."

I tried to say something, but all that came out were lisping sounds and escaping air, so I just gave up.

Evan screams and throws water on me, but the flames only grow stronger.

Vivian is also screaming and throwing everything from dirt to water on me, but what's the point?

The powder the Crown Prince gave me seems to be as effective as the potion—the flames show no sign of going out.

It truly seems like the fire won't extinguish until I'm reduced to ashes.

As my entire body burns away, I continue to shrink.

With the terrible smell, people vomiting, and water being splashed on me, I begin to lose consciousness.

My throat is dry.

As dry as my burning body.

That's why I bit off my tongue.

I once knew someone who was engulfed in flames.

I don't remember, or rather, I didn't want to remember.

Anyway, with clothes and hair completely burned away, only a wretched body remained as he died in agony.

What I saw then was a face with the tongue sticking out, like a dog lying on its back with its front paws curled up.

The contorted face was just a bonus.

So, I smiled.

Even though it feels like white foam is bubbling up inside me.

Strangely, it doesn't hurt.

I wanted to stand up and enjoy the tobacco scent, but that doesn't seem to be allowed.

I just knelt down.

Looking up at the sky as if in resentment.

I wonder if those who were tied to stakes and burned at the stake felt like this.

With fire burning at their feet, choking on rising smoke, looking up at the sky and praying to God.
data.epi_content3:
Please save me, or please kill me.

And God refused both.

Evan walks toward me, holding the piece of my tongue and continuously splashing water.

He's crying and begging me.

Pleading with his hands.

"...!?"

He's saying something, but I can't hear him clearly.

Still, he looks quite sad.

So I thought I might comfort him and tried to put my hand on his shoulder.

Then I realized my hand was on fire.

And since I couldn't move properly, I accidentally touched his face too.

It must have been hot and painful for Evan, but he just accepted my touch and let his shoulder start burning.

Perhaps he's confident that healing magic will somehow fix it.

What's the point? It must be incredibly painful.

But instead of mentioning the pain, he was just apologizing.

Like a devout believer who, after brutally slaughtering someone, confesses his sins earnestly when the priest arrives.

Trembling all over.

Well, burning to death is insane.

When just one arm was burned, the pain was unbearable.

Suffering and anguish are always inevitable for those with great self-awareness and profound emotions.

Suffering is life itself. Without suffering in life, how else would one find satisfaction?

That was the phrase constantly muttered by the person I mentioned earlier who burned to death—the one who always carried a book titled "Notes from Underground."

If that's the case, then I was ending my life, having escaped from being a beast.

The fire doesn't go out no matter how much water they pour, dirt they throw, or dust they stir up with the wind.

I found that fact quite pleasing.

In the distance, the Crown Prince watches with a slightly troubled expression.

Well, I guess even he is human after all.

I'm reminded of that conversation from before.

The one I had with the Crown Prince after shooting Lydia and her group, while catching my breath.

When he asked me to choose how I wanted to die, I thought he was crazy at first.

But it seems he just wanted to have a conversation with me before sending me off.

He must see his position as Crown Prince as a kind of job.

Vivian shows no expression, overlaying the past through me, her face hardened as she mechanically repeats the process of splashing water and throwing dirt.

Even though Evan has already realized it's useless and is just standing in front of me like this.

Vivian refuses to give up and is still trying to extinguish the fire somehow.

I can barely feel anything now, either because Vivian has stopped using magic or because all sensation has left my body.

My vision is gradually blurring.

My eyes must be nearly cooked.

Anyway, dying slowly in front of others was quite a new experience.

It was unpleasant, and I wouldn't want to go through it again, but it's not so bad that others are watching me and indirectly experiencing this pain and disgust.

My vision was already reddish, but now it's darkening.

I hope my corpse won't be mopped away like a rag.

I hope they'll remember my death as the ridiculous death of a wretched human being rather than the tragic death of a pet.

As always, in the final moment, I don't want to die.

Light up the red. I don't want to die in darkness.
